message.content:
You are reviewing the Angular frontend at C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend for PERFORMANCE and PAGE-LOAD issues only (a separate agent handles correctness bugs).

Focus on things that make pages load/render slower than they should:
- Change detection problems: missing OnPush, heavy work in templates, function calls / getters bound in templates that run every CD cycle, *ngFor without trackBy on large lists.
- RxJS: subscriptions not unsubscribed (memory leaks), nested subscribes, missing async pipe, duplicate HTTP calls, requests in loops, no caching/shareReplay where appropriate, sequential awaits that could be parallel.
- HTTP/data: N+1 request patterns, fetching more data than needed, no pagination, polling intervals that are too aggressive.
- Bundle / load: lack of lazy-loaded routes, large eager modules, big synchronous imports, missing virtual scrolling on big tables/grids (note: project uses ag-grid).
- Expensive synchronous work on init (large loops, JSON parsing) blocking render.

Search the src/ directory. Read actual code â€” don't guess. Ignore node_modules, dist, .angular.

Report the TOP findings ranked by impact. For each: severity (High/Med/Low), file path with line number (relative to C:\repos\dflow), a 1-2 sentence description of the problem, and a concrete fix. Be concrete and cite real code. Skip trivia. Return only the findings list, no preamble.

- deep-research: Deep research harness â€” fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly â€” if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files â€” merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way â€” as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup â€” install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path â€” even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") â€” and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highâ†’max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only â€” it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK â€” model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER â€” read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" â€” whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) â€” never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named â€” don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.text:
I have all the code. Here are the performance and page-load findings, ranked by impact.

## Findings

### 1. HIGH â€” `licensing_tracking.component.ts:495-523` & `itemLibrary.component.ts:594-614` â€” Subscriptions in ngOnInit never unsubscribed
`getLicenseFeedBacks({from:'POP'})` and `getLicenseFeedBacks({from:'Licensor'})` are subscribed in `ngOnInit` with raw `.subscribe()`, no `takeUntil(this.destroy$)`. Same pattern in itemLibrary `ItemDetailService.getLicenseFeedBacks(...)` (lines 594, 607). These leak on navigation away and back (these grids are navigated to/from constantly). Fix: `.pipe(takeUntil(this.destroy$)).subscribe(...)`.

### 2. HIGH â€” `itemLibrary.component.ts:215` + `defaultColDef` `onCellDoubleClicked: this.navigateToItemDetail` and getter `hoverTileGradient` bound in template
`itemLibrary.component.html:233` binds `[style.background]="hoverTileGradient"`, a getter (lines 1455-1463) that hashes a string in a loop on every change-detection cycle while a hover preview is shown. Also `buildImgUrl(...)` (line 231) is a method call in template. With default change detection (no OnPush) these re-run constantly. Fix: compute `hoverTileGradient`/img URL once in `showThumbnailPreview()` and store as a field.

### 3. HIGH â€” All four components: missing `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush`
None of the four `@Component` decorators set `changeDetection` (prod_tracking:36, itemLibrary:41, licensing_tracking:39, standardized:35). These are heavy ag-grid pages with template method/getter bindings (`getContextMenuItems`, `getRowStyle`, `hoverTileGradient`, `buildImgUrl`). Default CD runs the whole tree on every mouse/keyboard/timer event. Fix: add `changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` and drive updates via the existing `cdr` (already injected in itemLibrary).

### 4. HIGH â€” `prod_tracking.component.ts:174` (constructor) & `standardized.component.ts:241` (constructor) â€” HTTP call in constructor never unsubscribed, plus duplicate-ish init work
`getCustomGridLayoutList(...)` is subscribed in the constructor (runs before/independent of ngOnInit) with no cleanup. Constructor side-effect HTTP blocks nothing but leaks and fires even if the component is destroyed mid-flight. Fix: move to ngOnInit with `takeUntil`.

### 5. HIGH â€” `standardized.component.ts:374-419` â€” Nested sequential subscribes on init (getSTDItems â†’ getSTDHeader â†’ getSelectedGridView)
`ngOnInit` does `getSTDItems().subscribe(... getSTDHeader().subscribe(... getSelectedGridView().subscribe ...))` â€” three serialized round trips before the grid renders, none of them unsubscribed. `getSTDItems` and `getSTDHeader` are independent and could run in parallel (`forkJoin`), cutting page-load latency by one round trip. Fix: `forkJoin([getSTDItems, getSTDHeader])`, then apply view state; pipe `takeUntil`.

### 6. MED â€” `standardized.component.html:122` â€” `[rowData]="rowData"` with no `trackBy` / no `getRowId` mismatch, client-side row model with large data
Standardized uses the client-side row model (`[rowData]="rowData"`, no `serverSide`) and reassigns `this.rowData = data` on every change (lines 402, 587, 600, 751, etc.). `onBtnHighlight` (lines 570-588) mutates every row in a nested loop then calls `setGridOption('rowData', this.rowData)`, forcing a full grid refresh. `getRowId` is set (line 195) which helps, but the full-array replacement on highlight is expensive. Fix: use `applyTransaction({update:[...]})` for the changed rows instead of replacing the whole array.

### 7. MED â€” `licensing_tracking.component.ts:618-630` & `itemLibrary.component.ts:642-668` â€” Per-column work + `forkJoin` of N merch-group requests
itemLibrary fires one `getMerchGroup` HTTP per division (`requests = selectedDivIds.map(... getMerchGroup ...)`, lines 627-629) â€” acceptable via forkJoin, but the `valueGetter` built in `mapHeaderValues` (lines 706-741) does `groups.forEach` + `allMerchGroups.find(...)` **per cell render**. On a large grid this is O(cells Ă— groups) on every scroll/refresh. Fix: precompute a `Map<mg_id, mg_desc>` / `Map<mg_code,...>` once and look up by key in the valueGetter.

### 8. MED â€” `prod_tracking.component.ts:357`, `itemLibrary:579`, `licensing:480` â€” `setTimeout(... setGridOption("loading", true), 100)` and grid-ready races
Each ngOnInit schedules a `setTimeout` toggling loading, and licensing `onGridReady` (line 1204) adds `setTimeout(restoreStateFromSession, 500)`. These are minor but add scheduled work and a guaranteed 100ms/500ms delay to perceived readiness. Low cost individually; flag as Low-Med.

### 9. MED â€” `licensing_tracking.component.ts:1199` & `standardized.component.ts:1051` â€” `this.colHeaders.shift()` in onGridReady mutates the column-def array
`colHeaders.shift()` runs in `onGridReady` before colHeaders is populated by the async chain in some paths, and mutating the bound `columnDefs` array is a hidden cost / correctness-adjacent but also triggers column re-evaluation. Low perf impact; noting as Med because it runs on the bound array.

### 10. MED â€” Template `track` expressions use whole object instead of id
- `prod_tracking.html:15` `track viewLayout`, `:280` `track value`
- `itemLibrary.html:27` `track data` (divisionCode)
- `licensing.html:8` `track type`, `:97`(standardized) `track view`, `:73` `track data`, `:87` `track status`
These `@for` track the object reference, so any array replacement re-renders all options. Lists are small (dropdowns), so impact is Low-Med, but `track viewLayout.layout_name` / `track data.divCode_id` is the correct fix. (Note licensing:19 and itemLibrary:115 already correctly use `.layout_name`.)

### 11. LOW â€” `getContextMenuItems` / `getRowStyle` / `getRowId` passed as bare bindings in itemLibrary.html (lines 204-206)
`[getRowId]="getRowNodeId"`, `[getRowStyle]="getRowStyle"`, `[getContextMenuItems]="getContextMenuItems"` are property bindings re-read each CD cycle; they reference stable fields so cheap, but combined with no OnPush they participate in every CD pass. Resolved largely by finding #3.

### 12. LOW â€” `onCellValueChanged` in itemLibrary (lines 1144-1163) issues an extra full `getAllItem` fetch on every edit
Each cell edit triggers `getAllItem(...)` (to populate `oldRowData` for undo) **plus** `updateRfqItem`. That is a duplicate large fetch on every single cell change. Fix: maintain undo state from the in-memory row rather than re-fetching the whole dataset.

### Notes / non-issues
- All four correctly implement `ngOnDestroy` with `destroy$.next()/complete()`, and the `GridUISetupService.bootstrap(api, this.destroy$)` ties grid UI subscriptions to destroy â€” good. The leaks are specifically the `.subscribe()` calls that bypass `destroy$` (findings #1, #4).
- prod_tracking, itemLibrary, licensing use server-side row model with pagination (`paginationPageSize:100`, `cacheBlockSize:100`) â€” virtualization is fine there. Only standardized is client-side.
- Biggest page-load wins: parallelize standardized's init chain (#5) and add OnPush (#3); biggest leak fixes: #1 and #4.

I have everything I need. Here are the findings, ranked by impact.

## Performance & Page-Load Findings

### HIGH

**1. itemDetail: all init HTTP requests run sequentially instead of in parallel (slow page load)**
`C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\itemDetail\itemDetail.component.ts` lines 210â€“472 (`ngOnInit`).
The init awaits a long chain one-after-another: `getDivisionCode()` (215) â†’ `getSeasonCode` (221) â†’ `loadCustomerOptions()` (228) â†’ `getMerchGroup` subscribe (230), and inside that callback further sequential awaits `getCompanyCode` (420), `getAllVendors` (445), `getItemDepth()` (452), `getItemInfo()` (454). Most of these are independent. Each request blocks the next, multiplying total time-to-interactive. Fix: run independent calls with `forkJoin`/`Promise.all` (e.g. season, customers, company code, vendors, depth in parallel), only chaining what genuinely depends on `divisionCode` or `itemData`.

**2. itemDetail: `getMerchGroup` mixes `await` with `.subscribe`, and dependent calls live inside the subscribe callback**
Same file, line 230: `await this.itemService.getMerchGroup(...).subscribe(async (data) => {...})`. `await` on a Subscription is meaningless, and the heavy nested-loop processing (235â€“401) plus all downstream awaits run inside the callback, deepening the sequential chain and nesting `getCompanyCode`, `getUDFElements` subscribe (429), `getAllVendors`, `getItemInfo` etc. Fix: convert to `firstValueFrom`, hoist independent calls out, flatten the chain.

**3. itemDetail: `getItemInfo` (the item) is fetched twice on load**
Same file: `getDivisionCode()` (line 195) calls `this.ItemDetailService.getItemInfo({ id })` and stores `this.itemData`; then `getItemInfo()` (line 454) consumes that same `itemData`. But `openViewUploadedFilesDialog` (707) and the image-upload `(refreshItemData)="getItemInfo()"` re-fetch. More importantly, within init `getItemInfo()` then fires two more requests â€” `getMG` (566) and `GetLicensingStatus` (590) â€” serially after everything else. These should be parallelized with the main batch. Confirm the initial item fetch isn't duplicated by child components hitting the same endpoint.

**4. itemDetail HTML: expensive getters `timelineItems` and `teamMembers` bound in template, recomputed every change-detection cycle**
`itemDetail.component.html` line 100 (`@for (m of timelineItems...)`) and line 956 (`@for (member of teamMembers...)`). Both are getters in the .ts (lines 924 and 937) that build/map new arrays on every call. Because they return new array references, Angular's default CD re-renders these loops every cycle, and the getter bodies (`Date.now()`, `.map`) run constantly. Fix: compute once into a plain field when `itemData`/assignments change, or make them pure memoized values; ideally also adopt OnPush.

**5. license-tracking: N+1 / per-row work plus heavy synchronous processing in `updateRowData`**
`C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\itemDetail\license-tracking\license-tracking.component.ts` lines 149â€“208. For each row it calls `enrichRowWithGroupInfo` (325) which does `this.groupsList.find(...)` and, per group member, `this.usersList.find(...)` (332â€“335) â€” nested linear scans = O(rows Ă— members Ă— users). On large comment lists this is quadratic/cubic. Fix: build `Map` lookups for `groupsList` by id and `usersList` by id once, then O(1) lookups.

### MEDIUM

**6. No `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` on any reviewed component**
`itemDetail.component.ts` (32), `license-tracking.component.ts` (16), `art-piece.component.ts` (44), `art-piece-detail.component.ts` (23), `manage_users.component.ts` (29), `dashboard-analytics.component.ts` (11). All use default CD. Combined with template getters (#4) and heavy templates (itemDetail has hundreds of bindings across three attribute sets), this makes every async event re-evaluate the entire view. Fix: adopt OnPush where feasible, especially itemDetail and art-piece-detail.

**7. license-tracking: subscriptions never unsubscribed (memory leak)**
Same file. `getAllUsers` (101), `GetLicensingStatus` (109), `loadLicensingTimeline` (139), plus dialog/`updateRowData` follow-ups â€” all `.subscribe(...)` with no `takeUntil`/teardown and no `ngOnDestroy`. Component is created/destroyed each time the Licensing tab is opened, leaking. Fix: add `destroy$` + `takeUntil`, implement `ngOnDestroy`.

**8. itemDetail: subscriptions in init/`getItemInfo` not torn down**
`itemDetail.component.ts`: `getMerchGroup` (230), `getUDFElements` (429), `getMG` (566), `GetLicensingStatus` (590), `route.queryParams` (466) â€” none unsubscribed; no `ngOnDestroy`. The `route.queryParams` subscription in particular is a classic leak. Fix: `takeUntil(destroy$)` + `ngOnDestroy`.

**9. art-piece-detail: sequential awaits in `ngOnInit` + cascading re-fetch of full data after every mutation**
`C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\pages\art-piece\art-piece-detail\art-piece-detail.component.ts` lines 112â€“127: `getSeasonCode` â†’ `getCompanyCode` â†’ `getArtPieceInfo()` â†’ `getRelatedItems()` all awaited serially; season/company/art-piece are independent and should be `forkJoin`/`Promise.all`. Also every `setPrimaryImage` (301), `deleteImage` (330), `onUploadComplete` (382), `onSave` (415) calls `getArtPieceInfo()` which itself re-fetches art piece AND re-fetches `getMerchGroup` (line 145) â€” reloading the whole merch-group dataset just to refresh an image list. Fix: parallelize init; on image mutations refresh only attachments, not merch groups. Uses `.toPromise()` (deprecated per CLAUDE.md) at 117, 118, 131, 145, 265.

**10. art-piece-detail HTML: `getPrimaryImage(item)` function called in template inside `@for`**
`art-piece-detail.component.html` lines 306â€“307: `@if (getPrimaryImage(item))` then `[src]="getPrimaryImage(item)"` â€” the function (ts line 427, does `.find`) runs twice per related item per CD cycle. Fix: precompute a `primaryImageUrl` field on each related item in `getRelatedItems()`.

**11. art-piece: `convertToItem` does two `this.rowData.find(...)` scans for the same row**
`art-piece.component.ts` lines 748â€“749: `this.rowData.find(data => data.id === artPiece.id)` called twice back-to-back. Minor, but trivially dedupable into one lookup.

### LOW

**12. manage_users: HTTP call fired from constructor**
`manage_users.component.ts` line 177: `getCustomGridLayoutList(...).subscribe(...)` runs in the constructor (also not unsubscribed). Constructor-time I/O blocks component construction and runs before lifecycle. Fix: move to `ngOnInit`, add teardown.

**13. manage_users: `onBtExport` does sequential `await getBase64FromUrl` per row in a loop (N+1 fetches)**

**1. [High] Reference data (vendors / customers / RFQ groups) re-fetched on every component init, never cached/shareReplay'd**

Every RFQ/item/tracking page and every autocomplete re-downloads the full vendor & customer lists, producing many duplicate network round-trips on each navigation. CustomerService even has a `customers$` BehaviorSubject store (line 13) that most callers bypass.

Fix: Cache the token in memory (a service field updated on login/logout) and have the interceptor read the cached value, falling back to localStorage only once.

**3. [Med] AG Grid Enterprise registers ALL modules eagerly at app bootstrap**
`C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\app\app.module.ts:41-42` â€” `import { AllEnterpriseModule } from 'ag-grid-enterprise'; ModuleRegistry.registerModules([AllEnterpriseModule]);` runs at module-eval time. `AllEnterpriseModule` pulls the entire enterprise feature set into the initial bundle even on routes that never show a grid (login, factory form, notifications), bloating first-load JS.

These are only needed on specific feature pages (comments editor, calendar views, carousels) but are paid for on initial load of every route including `/login`.
Fix: Move `QuillModule`, `CalendarModule`, `CarouselModule`, and the moment date adapter into the lazy feature modules that use them, keeping the root bundle lean.

`C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\@vex\layout\toolbar\toolbar-notifications\toolbar-notifications.component.ts:49` and `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend\src\@vex\layout\toolbar\toolbar-user\toolbar-user.component.ts:55` â€” `popoverRef.afterClosed$.subscribe(...)` is called on each open with no `takeUntil`/`untilDestroyed` and no stored subscription. ## Performance & Page-Load Findings â€” designflow-frontend

Ranked by impact. All paths relative to `C:\repos\dflow`.

### HIGH

**1. RFQ init is a serialized HTTP waterfall, not parallel** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts:658` (`ngOnInit`) â†’ `loadRFQStatuses():745`. `ngOnInit` chains `verifyRole` â†’ `getRFQItemStatus`, and only *inside* that subscribe (lines 750-756) does it fire `getRFQGroup`, `loadCustomers`, `loadDeliveryLocations`, `loadRFQSteps`, then `afterMetadataReady()` â†’ `getAllRFQItem()`. These reference lists are independent but run sequentially behind one another, multiplying time-to-grid on the app's heaviest page. **Fix:** `forkJoin` the independent reference calls (status, group, customers, delivery, steps, CBM, license) and only chain `getAllRFQItem` after; this is the largest page-load win.

**2. RFQ subscriptions never unsubscribed (leak)** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` â€” `takeUntil` count is 0 despite ~30 `.subscribe()` calls (lines 746, 762, 815, 825, 843, 850, 857, 864, 880, 943, 4086, 4103, â€¦). `destroy$` is declared and completed in `ngOnDestroy:401` but never piped into any stream. Every navigation to/from RFQ leaks all these subscriptions. **Fix:** add `.pipe(takeUntil(this.destroy$))` to every init/data subscription.

**3. itemDetail init is fully serialized + meaningless `await` on a Subscription** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemDetail/itemDetail.component.ts:210-472`. Chains `getDivisionCode` â†’ `getSeasonCode` â†’ `loadCustomerOptions` â†’ `getMerchGroup` (line 230 does `await ...subscribe(...)`, which is a no-op await) and nests `getCompanyCode`, `getAllVendors`, `getItemDepth`, `getItemInfo` inside callbacks. Independent calls run one-after-another. Also `getItemInfo` is effectively fetched twice (line 195 stores `itemData`, line 454 reconsumes, plus child re-fetches). **Fix:** `firstValueFrom` + `forkJoin` the independent calls; flatten the chain; dedupe the item fetch.

**4. Detail-cell-renderer refetches static reference data per row expansion (N+1)** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/detail-cell-renderer.component.ts:131-142`. Each expanded RFQ row instantiates a renderer that calls `getFOBCountry`, `loadDetailColumnHeaders`, and `getFactoryCountryMap` â€” global static data fetched once per expanded row. **Fix:** cache these via `shareReplay(1)` in the service so expansions reuse a single response.

**5. Reference data (vendors/customers/RFQ groups/company codes) never cached** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts:63,75,105,165` and `customer.service.ts:23`. These raw `http.get` methods are called from ~70 sites (every grid page + every autocomplete) with no `shareReplay`; `CustomerService` even has a `customers$` store (line 13) that callers bypass. Full vendor/customer lists re-download on each navigation. **Fix:** expose memoized `shareReplay({bufferSize:1, refCount:false})` observables; route callers through them / the existing `customers$` store.

**6. standardized.component init is nested serial subscribes + client-side full-array rebuilds** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/standardized/standardized.component.ts:374-419`. `getSTDItems â†’ getSTDHeader â†’ getSelectedGridView` nested serially (3 round trips, none unsubscribed) before render; first two are independent. Uses client-side row model and `onBtnHighlight` (570-588) mutates every row then replaces the whole `rowData` array. **Fix:** `forkJoin` the independent calls + `takeUntil`; use `applyTransaction({update})` instead of full array replacement.

**7. Expensive getters/functions bound in templates (re-run every CD cycle)** â€” itemDetail.component.html:100,956 bind getters `timelineItems`/`teamMembers` (ts:924,937) that build new arrays each call; itemLibrary.component.html:233 binds getter `hoverTileGradient` (ts:1455) that hashes a string in a loop; art-piece-detail.component.html:306 calls `getPrimaryImage(item)` (`.find`) twice per related item. With default change detection these run on every event. **Fix:** precompute into fields when source data changes.

### MEDIUM

**8. No OnPush anywhere on heavy grid pages** â€” only 8 of 278 components use `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush`. rfq, prod_tracking, itemLibrary, licensing_tracking, standardized, itemDetail, art-piece(-detail), manage_users all use default CD with method/getter bindings (`getContextMenuItems`, `getRowStyle`, the getters in #7). Every mouse/keyboard/timer event re-evaluates the whole tree. **Fix:** adopt OnPush + drive updates through the already-injected `cdr`.

**9. AG Grid Enterprise registers ALL modules at bootstrap** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/app.module.ts:41-42` `ModuleRegistry.registerModules([AllEnterpriseModule])` at module-eval pulls the entire enterprise bundle into initial load even on `/login`, `/notifications`, factory form. **Fix:** register only the modules used, or move registration into lazy grid feature modules.

**10. Heavy third-party modules eagerly imported in root AppModule** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/app.module.ts:82-98` eagerly imports `QuillModule.forRoot`, `CalendarModule.forRoot` (+date-fns), `CarouselModule`, `FontAwesomeModule`, `MatMomentDateModule`. Only needed on specific pages but paid for on every initial load. **Fix:** move into the lazy modules that use them.

**11. license-tracking: cubic per-row enrichment + leaks** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemDetail/license-tracking/license-tracking.component.ts:149-208,325-335`. `enrichRowWithGroupInfo` does `groupsList.find` then per-member `usersList.find` â†’ O(rows Ă— members Ă— users). Subscriptions (101,109,139) have no teardown and no `ngOnDestroy`. **Fix:** build `Map` lookups once; add `destroy$`/`takeUntil`.

**12. itemLibrary: full dataset re-fetched on every cell edit** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemLibrary/itemLibrary.component.ts:1144-1163`. `onCellValueChanged` calls `getAllItem(...)` (to build undo state) plus `updateRfqItem` on every single edit â€” a large duplicate fetch per keystroke-commit. **Fix:** derive undo state from the in-memory row.

**13. art-piece-detail: serial init + over-broad refetch on mutations** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/art-piece/art-piece-detail/art-piece-detail.component.ts:112-127`. `getSeasonCode â†’ getCompanyCode â†’ getArtPieceInfo â†’ getRelatedItems` awaited serially (first three independent); every image mutation (setPrimary/delete/upload/save) calls `getArtPieceInfo` which re-fetches the whole merch-group dataset (line 145). Uses deprecated `.toPromise()` (117,118,131,145,265). **Fix:** parallelize init; refresh only attachments on image ops.

**14. Subscriptions/HTTP fired from constructors, never torn down** â€” `prod_tracking.component.ts:174`, `standardized.component.ts:241`, `manage_users.component.ts:177` all call `getCustomGridLayoutList(...).subscribe(...)` in the constructor with no cleanup. **Fix:** move to ngOnInit + `takeUntil`.

**15. manage_users destroys/recreates the entire grid on filter change** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/manage_users/manage_users.component.ts:899-905`. `onUserTypeChange` toggles `isGridVisible` off then on via `setTimeout(...,0)`, tearing down and rebuilding the whole grid instead of reloading rows. Also `destroy$` (line 104) is never completed, so `gridUISetupService.bootstrap` teardown (737) never fires. **Fix:** reload data only; complete `destroy$` in `ngOnDestroy`.

**16. itemLibrary merch-group valueGetter does linear scans per cell** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/itemLibrary/itemLibrary.component.ts:706-741`. The `valueGetter` from `mapHeaderValues` runs `allMerchGroups.find(...)` per cell on every scroll/refresh â†’ O(cells Ă— groups). **Fix:** precompute a `Map` keyed by id/code.

### LOW

**17. Export loops fetch images one-at-a-time (N+1)** â€” `manage_users.component.ts:612-617` and `art-piece.component.ts:1011-1023` `await getBase64FromUrl` per row in a loop. Export-time only. **Fix:** `Promise.all`.

**18. UserAuthInterceptor reads localStorage synchronously per request** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/app/helpers/interceptor/user-auth.interceptor.ts:15`. `localStorage.getItem('token')` on every outbound request (mirrored in notification.service.ts:47, main.service.ts:16/22). Adds up under parallel grid requests. **Fix:** cache token in memory, update on login/logout.

**19. Toolbar popover `afterClosed$` subscriptions leak per interaction** â€” `designflow-frontend/src/@vex/layout/toolbar/toolbar-notifications/toolbar-notifications.component.ts:49` and `toolbar-user/toolbar-user.component.ts:55`. No `take(1)`/`untilDestroyed` on the long-lived singleton toolbar. **Fix:** `take(1)`.

**20. `@for` track-by-object on dropdown/option lists** â€” across prod_tracking, itemLibrary, standardized, art-piece, itemDetail templates (e.g. `track value`, `track data`, `track option`). Tracking object references defeats reuse when arrays are re-sorted/recreated. Lists are small, so low impact. **Fix:** `track x.id`.

### Notes (verified non-issues)
- Routing is fully lazy-loaded with `QuicklinkStrategy` preloading â€” good. Note `onSameUrlNavigation: 'reload'` (app-routing.module.ts:364) forces full re-resolution on same-URL nav.
- `NotificationService` polling (30s, `exhaustMap`+`startWith`, proper `ngOnDestroy`) is well-built â€” only nit is no pause-on-tab-hidden and `getMentionableUsers` isn't cached.
- prod_tracking/itemLibrary/licensing use server-side row model with pagination â€” virtualization is fine; only `standardized` is client-side. `custom-layout`/`layout` correctly use `@UntilDestroy`.
